message:
test(gui/quad): cover cell→vertex grid math with adjacency + origin invariants (ft-1memj.28/A, ft-nmmlt)

The existing test_cell_vertex_generation pinned a single sentinel cell
(row=3, col=5). That catches gross sign/scale bugs but not the
invariants the renderer actually depends on:

* row 0, col 0 must land at the configured origin
* horizontally-adjacent cells must share their vertical edge
  (otherwise glyph columns grow seams)
* vertically-adjacent cells must share their horizontal edge
  (otherwise text rows show hairlines)
* a non-zero origin (tab-bar height, scrollbar gutter) must translate
  every cell by the same vector

Add a small `cell_vertex_positions` helper that mirrors the production
formula `pos = origin + cell_index * cell_size` and four targeted unit
tests built on it, plus a 3x4 grid walk that exercises the formula
across every (row, col) in a small matrix instead of one point.

All run on the existing #[cfg(test)] mod in quad.rs; no GPU required.
Verified via:

  rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-cc6-target \
    CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang \
    CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ \
    cargo test -p frankenterm-gui cell_vertex

6 tests pass (the original + 5 new). Closes the smallest unit-test row
in ft-1memj.28's GPU-unrequired list.
